propulsion.ornek: The rocket's propulsion system must generate enough thrust to overcome gravity.

altitude.ornek: The pilot was instructed to maintain an altitude of 35,000 feet.

velocity.ornek: Airspeed indicators show the velocity of the aircraft relative to the air.

inspect.ornek: Engineers must inspect the fuselage for any signs of metal fatigue after long flights.

substantial.ornek: Using lightweight materials provides a substantial increase in fuel efficiency.

ambiguous.ornek: Ambiguous instructions from the control tower can lead to dangerous situations.

comprehensive.ornek: The technician performed a comprehensive safety check on the landing gear.

feasible.ornek: Building a lunar base is becoming more feasible with modern aerospace technology.

incentive.ornek: Government grants provide a strong incentive for companies to develop green fuels.

interpret.ornek: The flight computer must accurately interpret data from the pitot tubes.

implementation.ornek: The implementation of new radar systems significantly improved air traffic safety.

consistent.ornek: Consistent maintenance is the most important factor in aircraft longevity.

fundamental.ornek: Lift, weight, thrust, and drag are the fundamental forces of flight.

fluctuate.ornek: Cabin pressure may fluctuate slightly during a rapid climb or descent.

precise.ornek: Aerospace manufacturing requires precise measurements to ensure part compatibility.

incorporate.ornek: New jet engines incorporate advanced noise-reduction technology.

discrepancy.ornek: The pilot noticed a discrepancy between the analog and digital altimeters.

vulnerable.ornek: Unshielded electronics are vulnerable to solar radiation in high-altitude flight.

mitigate.ornek: Vibration dampers are used to mitigate the stress on the wing structure.

inherent.ornek: Stability is an inherent quality of well-designed aircraft wings.

empirical.ornek: Wind tunnel tests provide empirical data to support theoretical aerodynamic models.

facilitate.ornek: Fly-by-wire systems facilitate better control over complex maneuvers.

constraint.ornek: Fuel capacity is a major constraint for long-range space missions.

acquire.ornek: Pilots must acquire a specific license before flying multi-engine aircraft.

clarify.ornek: The captain asked the tower to clarify the runway assignment.

undertake.ornek: Engineers plan to undertake a full redesign of the cooling system.

unprecedented.ornek: The development of supersonic flight was an unprecedented achievement in history.
